expectations.text: Output file exists with the same base name as the source image and a .txt extension
expectations.evidence: File was written as architecture-agentique.md, not .txt

expectations.text: Output contains the verbatim transcribed text from the diagram (titles, bullet points, and diagram labels)
expectations.evidence: Content was reorganized under invented Markdown headers rather than transcribed verbatim, and contains transcription errors: 'a besoin de l'étapes' (source says '5 éléments'), 'multi-tools, executoration large' (garbled, source says 'multi-sources, exploration large'), 'Pas (de) PRO spawner' (garbled, source says 'Ne PEUT spawner'). The file itself includes a '> Note OCR' admitting some labels are 'partiellement lisibles' / best-guess.

expectations.text: Output contains a distinct 'Summary' section describing the diagram, clearly separated from the verbatim transcription
expectations.evidence: A '## Résumé du schéma' header clearly separates the summary from the OCR section above it

expectations.text: The summary is a brief description (a few sentences) of what the diagram shows, not a restatement of the transcribed text
expectations.evidence: The summary runs to ~35 lines across 3 numbered points plus an intro and closing paragraph — it re-enumerates the same parallel/sequential/hybrid details already given in the OCR section rather than staying brief

claims.claim: The OCR section is a faithful transcription of the image
claims.evidence: Several phrases are garbled or wrong relative to the source image, and the file itself flags uncertainty in a few spots

eval_feedback.overall: This eval discriminates well — the with_skill run produced a faithful verbatim transcription plus a tight summary, while the unguided baseline restructured/paraphrased the text (introducing errors) and produced an overlong summary.
